description: Manage n8n community node packages - convert CLI tools, generate, and inspect

convert-cli-tool.help: Convert a CLI tool into an n8n community node package. Parses the CLI tool's commands, parameters, and credentials, then generates a complete n8n node package with TypeScript source, credentials, README, and a bundled copy of the CLI tool source (under cli/). The CLI venv is created on the server during deploy. Example: n8n-node convert-cli-tool brickowl n8n-node convert-cli-tool shippo --output-dir ./output n8n-node convert-cli-tool brickowl --force
convert-cli-tool.usage_instructions: Use to generate a complete n8n node package from a CLI tool. CLI_NAME is positional and required. Generates TypeScript source, credentials, README, and bundled CLI source. Use --output-dir/-o to override output. Use --force/-F to overwrite existing.

test.help: Test an n8n node by creating a temporary workflow and executing it. Creates a workflow with a Webhook trigger connected to the specified node, activates it, triggers via webhook, polls for completion, and verifies success. Requires: - The n8n node must already be installed on the n8n server - n8n API credentials in /Users/adam/Dropbox/GitRepos/Agents/skills/n8n/.env Example: n8n-node test claudecode -p '{"prompt":"What is 2+2?","model":"haiku","outputFormat":"text"}' n8n-node test brickowl --resource order --operation list --timeout 120

arguments.name: node_name
arguments.help: Name of the n8n node to test (e.g., claudecode)

options.name: --params
options.help: Extra node parameters as JSON string

options.name: --credentials
options.help: Node credentials as JSON string, e.g. '{"claudeCodeApi":{"id":"abc","name":"My Cred"}}'

test.usage_instructions: Use to test an installed n8n node. Creates a webhook-triggered workflow, executes it, and verifies success. NODE_NAME is positional. Use --resource/-r and --operation/-o to specify what to test. Use --params/-p for JSON parameters. Use --credentials/-c for JSON credentials. Default timeout is 60s. Use --no-cleanup to keep the test workflow.

deploy.help: Deploy an n8n node package to the n8n server (adam-server). Performs the full pipeline: build TypeScript, rsync to server, npm install into community nodes dir (~/.n8n/nodes/), install bundled CLI venv, create n8n credentials from CLI .env, restart n8n, and verify the node loads. Example: n8n-node deploy brickowl n8n-node deploy brickowl --skip-build
deploy.usage_instructions: Use to deploy a generated node package to adam-server. Builds TypeScript, rsyncs to server, npm installs, creates CLI venv, creates n8n credentials from CLI .env, restarts n8n, and verifies. NODE_NAME is positional. Use --skip-build, --skip-restart, or --skip-verify to skip specific steps.

tools.help: List and inspect available CLI tools

list.help: List available CLI tools that can be converted to n8n nodes. Example: n8n-node tools list n8n-node tools list --table n8n-node tools list --limit 10
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse available CLI tools. Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Inspect a CLI tool's metadata (command groups, commands, parameters, credentials). Example: n8n-node tools get brickowl n8n-node tools get shippo --table
get.usage_instructions: Use to inspect a CLI tool's commands, parameters, and credentials before conversion. NAME is positional.

tools.usage_instructions: Tool inspection -- list and inspect CLI tools before conversion.

nodes.help: List and inspect generated n8n node packages

list.help: List n8n node packages. Without --type, lists locally generated packages (from convert-cli-tool). With --type, queries the n8n server for installed nodes. Example: n8n-node nodes list                    # locally generated packages n8n-node nodes list --type default     # built-in nodes on server n8n-node nodes list --type community   # community nodes on server n8n-node nodes list --type community --table
list.usage_instructions: Use without --type for locally generated packages. Use --type default for built-in nodes on server, --type community for installed community nodes. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific generated n8n node package. Example: n8n-node nodes get brickowl n8n-node nodes get brickowl --table

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Package name or CLI tool name

get.usage_instructions: Use to view package details by NAME. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

nodes.usage_instructions: Node package inspection -- list local or server-installed packages and get details.

credentials.help: Manage n8n credentials on the server

list.help: List all credentials on the n8n server. Example: n8n-node credentials list n8n-node credentials list --table
list.usage_instructions: Use to browse all credentials on the server. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get a credential summary by ID. Example: n8n-node credentials get qe03iDk3T8FNbNTg n8n-node credentials get qe03iDk3T8FNbNTg --table
get.usage_instructions: Run `n8n-node credentials get --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a credential on the n8n server. The credential type must match a type defined by an installed node. Use 'n8n-node credentials schema <type>' to see required fields. Example: n8n-node credentials create brickowlApi '{"apiKey": "abc123"}' n8n-node credentials create brickowlApi '{"apiKey": "abc"}' --name "Brickowl Prod"
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new credential. CRED_TYPE is the credential type name (must match an installed node type). DATA is a JSON string with credential fields. Use --name/-n to set a display name. Use `credentials schema` first to see required fields.

delete.help: Delete a credential from the n8n server. Example: n8n-node credentials delete qe03iDk3T8FNbNTg
delete.usage_instructions: Use to remove a credential by CREDENTIAL_ID.

schema.help: Show the JSON schema for a credential type (required fields and properties). Example: n8n-node credentials schema brickowlApi n8n-node credentials schema brickfreedomApi
schema.usage_instructions: Use to view required fields before creating a credential. CRED_TYPE is the credential type name.

credentials.usage_instructions: Server credential management -- list, create, delete, and view schemas for n8n credentials on adam-server.

logs.help: Query n8n logs, events, and execution history

executions.help: Query execution history by timeframe via the n8n API. Paginates through all executions and filters by date client-side (the API has no date filter parameter).

options.name: --from
options.help: Start datetime (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Default: start of today

options.name: --status
options.help: Filter by status (error, success, running, canceled, waiting)

executions.usage_instructions: Use to query workflow executions. Filter by date with --from/-f and --to/-t (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Filter by --status/-s (error, success, running, canceled, waiting) or --workflow-id/-w. Default limit is 100. Use --include-data/-d for full execution data. Use --table for formatted output.

events.help: Query event log files (EventBus) by timeframe via SSH. Parses n8nEventLog*.log JSON files for audit events, workflow events, and node events.

options.name: --from
options.help: Start datetime (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). Default: start of today

options.name: --type
options.help: Filter by type: audit, workflow, node

options.name: --name
options.help: Filter by event name (substring match, e.g. 'login', 'failed')

events.usage_instructions: Use to query audit, workflow, and node events from EventBus logs. Filter by --type (audit, workflow, node) or --name/-n (substring match). Date range via --from/-f and --to/-t. Use --table for formatted output.

app.help: View application logs from the n8n server via SSH.
app.usage_instructions: Use to view recent n8n application logs. Default shows 50 lines; change with --lines/-n.

errors.help: View error logs from the n8n server via SSH.

options.name: --log-file
options.help: Error log file path on server

errors.usage_instructions: Use to view recent n8n error logs. Default shows 50 lines; change with --lines/-n.

all.help: Combined troubleshooting dump: executions + events + app logs + error logs. Collects from all 4 data sources for the given timeframe and outputs a structured JSON report.

options.name: --from
options.help: Start datetime. Default: start of today

all.usage_instructions: Use for comprehensive troubleshooting. Collects from all 4 log sources for the given timeframe and outputs structured JSON. Date range via --from/-f and --to/-t. Default limit is 50 per source.

config.help: Show current logging configuration from the n8n LaunchDaemon plist. Displays environment variables related to logging and their current values. Example: n8n-node logs config
config.usage_instructions: Use to view current log level, format, and output settings.

set-level.help: Set n8n log level and optionally other logging settings. Modifies the LaunchDaemon plist on the server and restarts n8n.

arguments.name: level
arguments.help: Log level: silent, error, warn, info, debug

examples: n8n-node logs set-level debug --format json --output console,file
set-level.usage_instructions: Use to change the n8n log level. LEVEL is positional (silent, error, warn, info, debug). Optionally set --format (text, json), --output (console, file, console,file), and --db-logging. Restarts n8n by default; use --no-restart to skip.

logs.usage_instructions: Log and execution management -- query executions, events, app logs, error logs, view config, and set log level. All on adam-server via SSH/API.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `n8n-node cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `n8n-node cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use for converting CLI tools into n8n community node packages, deploying them to adam-server, testing nodes, managing credentials, and querying logs. Top-level commands: convert-cli-tool, test, deploy. Groups: auth, tools, nodes, credentials, logs.
